ip_outcome_note: PokemonTCG.io API confirms card name 'Passimian', National Pokedex no. 766, Fighting-type, Alola region. Art depicts grey-white primate with leaf-pattern head marking near a tree, consistent with canonical Passimian appearance. IP is Game Freak/Nintendo-internal (Pokemon franchise). NOTE: Passimian is NOT part of the elemental-monkey trio (Pansage/Pansear/Panpour); it is a separate species introduced in Gen 7.

trivia_bullets.text: Darkness Ablaze (SWSH3) released August 14, 2020, the third expansion of the Sword & Shield TCG series. The set is dominated by Charizard VMAX as its headline card; Passimian appears as a common Fighting-type at no. 97/189 [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3; WebSearch: pokeflip.com Darkness Ablaze set context].

Passimian (National Pokedex no. 766) is a Fighting-type introduced in Pokemon Sun (Gen 7, 2016). It is exclusive to Pokemon Sun and Ultra Sun in the Alola games, and to Pokemon Sword in Sword & Shield — its counterpart Oranguru is the Moon/Shield exclusive. Passimian is NOT part of the elemental-monkey trio (Pansage, Pansear, Panpour); it is a wholly independent species classified as the Teamwork Pokemon [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-97; WebSearch: pokemondb.net/sun-moon/exclusive].

The name combines 'pass' (as in ball-passing) and 'simian' (primate). Its design is explicitly modeled on American football: the round coconut-like helmet, broad shoulders, and heavy emphasis on group-play mechanics. French and German translations reinforce this — the French name 'Quartermac' and German 'Quartermak' both reference the quarterback position [WebSearch: Bulbapedia Passimian (Pokemon); WebSearch: pokemaniacal.com/2018/09/15/passimian/].

"Displaying amazing teamwork, they follow the orders of their boss as they all help out in the search for their favorite berries." — card flavor text. The boss of a Passimian troop earns that role by throwing a berry the farthest; techniques are passed from boss to group across generations [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-97; WebSearch: Bulbapedia Passimian].

The card's first attack, Call for Family, searches for up to 2 Basic Pokemon for the Bench — a strong setup move mechanically consistent with Passimian's lore identity as a troop-builder. The second attack, Rock Hurl (70 damage, ignores Resistance), mirrors the berry-throwing combat behavior described in the Pokedex [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-97].
